It was the one that talked about Malcolm X"). The Malcolm X book reference is likely *The Fire Next Time* (1963) or *One Day, When I Was Lost* (1972 screenplay Baldwin wrote on Malcolm X)

pending_context.candidate_correction: (uncertain — possibly *The Primer* by Walter, or "primer" as common noun)
pending_context.notes: Speaker references "this book, Alex Premma" near the end. Could be a Walter children's book titled *Alec's Primer* (the 2004 Walter children's book about the canonical slave-child-learns-to-read story). The 2004 timing suggests this might post-date the interview's 2013 date but Walter could have written/been writing it earlier. Worth verifying

pending_context.whisper: "the African Festival of Culture" in Nigeria
pending_context.notes: Speaker: "I had been asked to go with a group of artists and writers and sculptors to Nigeria, to the African Festival of Culture, where all people from the diaspora were coming. And I was the only children's book writer." The canonical Nigerian Black-diaspora arts festival is FESTAC '77 (15 Jan - 12 Feb 1977 in Lagos and Kaduna), which drew ~17,000 participants from 56 countries — including a US delegation of writers, sculptors, and visual artists organized by Andrew Young / Jeff Donaldson. Walter's account ("only children's book writer in the US delegation") aligns canonically. Missed by Pass 1/2/3. Significant: dates Walter's Ghana / Elmina visit to 1977 (post-FESTAC).

pending_context.whisper: "She's a cacuey woman... She might be a chola" (Ghanaian market identification of Walter)
pending_context.candidate_correction: (uncertain — likely Akan ethnic-group names: possibly "Kwahu" / "Krobo" or "Fante" / "Ga"; possibly "Kweku" Akan day-name)
pending_context.notes: Speaker recounts being identified by Ghanaian market-women: "She's a cacuey woman. They said no, I don't think she's cacuey. She might be a chola." These are Ghanaian Akan / Twi ethnic-identifier terms that Whisper has rendered phonetically. "Cacuey" may be "Kwahu" (an Akan subgroup of the Eastern Region) or "Kweku" (the Akan male day-name for Wednesday-born); "chola" may be "Krobo" (Ga-Adangme ethnic group) or "Cholua" (no canonical match). Flag for adversarial review by a West-African-linguistics specialist.

pending_context.whisper: "old back in the thirties" (sister's husband moved to WA)
pending_context.candidate_correction: way back in the thirties" or "all the way back in the thirties
pending_context.notes: Speaker: "My sister had gone there. Her husband had moved there, old back in the thirties." Whisper renders "way back" or "all the way back" as "old back" — a class of pre-WWII chronological-marker degradations. Pre-dates Walter's own 1944 WA arrival by ~5-10 years. Useful chronological grounding.

pending_context.whisper: "I did names there in the camp" (LA Times book reviews context)
pending_context.candidate_correction: (uncertain — likely "I did names there in the can" or "I did them there in the camp" or possibly "I did names there in the canon" — meaning Walter listed canonical Black writers she reviewed)
pending_context.notes: Speaker: "And I was able to do names there in the camp. The guy who was in prison in South Africa. Mandela. Mandela's book." The "names there in the camp" phrase is contextually anomalous — speaker is transitioning from listing Du Bois books to Mandela. Possible degradations: "I did names there in the can[on]" (canonical writers), "I did them there in the camp" (where "camp" is some indeterminate prior reference), or simple Whisper noise. Flag for adversarial review.

pending_context.candidate_correction: Honor Award" or simply "the Coretta Scott King Award
pending_context.notes: Speaker: "I had to make a choice. Did I go on this walk? Would I stay and go to San Francisco and receive that Honorable Award?" The Coretta Scott King Award presentation is at the annual American Library Association (ALA) conference banquet; the 1986 ALA was held in New York (not San Francisco). Worth checking whether Walter is confusing venues or whether the 1986 ALA had a satellite San Francisco event. Adjacent finding: she received the SCBWI / regional presentation in San Francisco rather than the ALA banquet. Flag for verification.
